title: viola praemorsa subsp. major
intro: Viola praemorsa subsp. major is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, commonly known as pansy or heartsease. The medicinal properties of Viola praemorsa subsp. major are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, colds, and fever. In herbal preparations, Viola praemorsa subsp. major can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, soothe sore throats, and support immune function.
